description_long: This is a heavy-duty sledgehammer featuring a robust, rectangular black metal head with slightly beveled edges and a central wooden handle. The head shows signs of wear, with visible scuff marks and some areas where the paint has chipped, revealing the underlying metal. The handle is made of natural wood, displaying a straight, cylindrical form with a slightly thicker grip at the end for better handling. The wood grain is visible, and the handle appears to be securely fitted into the head, with no visible fasteners or additional attachments. The hammer's head is solid and designed for delivering powerful blows, suitable for demolition or driving stakes. The overall appearance suggests it is a utilitarian tool, likely used in construction or maintenance tasks in urban environments.
description: A sturdy sledgehammer with a worn black metal head and a straight wooden handle, showing signs of heavy use. The tool is robust and built for demanding tasks like demolition or driving stakes.
description_view_0: Front view showing the broad, flat striking face of the black metal head and the end of the wooden handle.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the straight wooden handle and the rectangular profile of the hammer head.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hammer head and the rounded end of the wooden handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the length of the handle and the rectangular head, with visible wear on the metal.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a natural finish, slightly thickened at the grip end, smooth with visible wood grain and no additional grip enhancements.
attribute_head_type: Rectangular sledgehammer head made of black-painted steel, with flat striking faces and slightly beveled edges, designed for heavy impact.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible scuff marks, paint chipping, and surface scratches on the metal head; handle appears slightly worn but intact.
